creator: freezr@disroot.org
text:
Dear Maintainer,

this is has been affecting me for a while, and I noticed that is shared among other users:

https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/vboxvideo-driver-needs-a-bump.86128/
https://forums.freebsd.org/threads/black-screen-when-x-starts-after-upgrade.86237/page-2

My host is Win10 PRO 64Bit, Unfortunately I couldn't find any meaningful logs for troubleshooting it: the screen goes black and it looks like nothing is recorded anywhere therefore I was only able to reproduce the bug:

1. Download https://download.freebsd.org/releases/amd64/amd64/ISO-IMAGES/13.1/FreeBSD-13.1-RELEASE-amd64-bootonly.iso.xz
2. Extract
3. Create a new virtual machine (FreeBSD 64Bit): PAE/NX & Nested VT-x/AMD-V enabled; Paravirtualization interface: default; nested paging enabled.
4. Installation from ISO: basic installation, with clear ZFS filesystem, 64GB (dynamic) disk, 2GB Ram, 2GB Swap, 64MB Video Mem, 2 vCPU.
5. # pkg upgrade and then pkg install xorg virtualbox-ose-additions
6. Modify RC.CONF: vboxguest_enable="YES" \ vboxservice_enable="YES" \ vboxservice_flags="--disable-timesync"
7. reboot, login, startx = works!
8. Modifiy repo into latest as described here: https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/book/#quarterly-latest-branch
9. # pkg upgrade: it will update among the others xorg and virtualbox-ose-addition.
10: reboot; loging; startx: black screen.

For any additional request please  do not hesitate to ask!

Thanks,

Freezr (ex Gnuserland)
